reason: Restricted throughout the program; high glycemic load not aligned with weight-loss goals.
conditions: Always restricted, every week.

reason: To be avoided per general program guidelines.
conditions: Always restricted.

reason: Excluded from general vegetable sauté preparations.
conditions: Excluded specifically from the general sauteed-veggies recipe; standalone raw banana intake also generally discouraged.

reason: To be avoided per general program guidelines; jaggery used sparingly as an occasional alternative.
conditions: Always restricted.

reason: To be avoided per general program guidelines; also flagged as a bad food combination when paired with ghee.
conditions: Always restricted.

reason: High-GI refined flour, to be avoided.
conditions: Always restricted; explicitly called out in Week 11.

reason: To be restricted/minimized; brown rice, semi-brown rice, millets, quinoa, or couscous preferred.
conditions: Restricted generally; explicitly noted in Weeks 5 and 9 ('restrict potato, white rice').

reason: To reduce consumption of starchy root vegetables.
conditions: General reduction recommended, not a full restriction.

food: Packaged/Processed Food (biscuits, cookies, chips, instant foods, bakery items)
reason: Processed and packaged foods are not allowed; contradicts the 'foods filled with life' principle.
conditions: Always restricted, with the narrow exception of occasional marie biscuits with evening tea or khakra as a snack.

reason: To be avoided, including on a designated cheat meal.
conditions: Always restricted, even on cheat days.

reason: To be avoided when eating out / on cheat meals.
conditions: Restricted specifically during eating-out / cheat-meal scenarios.

reason: To be avoided alongside maida, sweets, and sugars.
conditions: Always restricted; explicitly noted in Week 11.

reason: Avoid eating these specific foods at night, per traditional food-timing guidance.
conditions: Restricted specifically for dinner/nighttime consumption only; permitted at other times of day.

food: Milk combined with Meat, Fish, or Eggs
reason: Considered a bad food combination that weakens digestive fire.
conditions: Avoid combining these items in the same meal. Note: meat/fish/eggs are non-applicable in this fully vegetarian dataset but the combining principle is preserved from the source for completeness.

reason: Considered a bad food combination.
conditions: Non-applicable in this fully vegetarian dataset (eggs have been substituted); preserved from source for completeness.

reason: Considered a bad food combination that weakens digestive fire.
conditions: Avoid combining; explicitly reinforced in the chia pudding recipe ('don't add fruits if you are adding normal milk').

reason: Considered a bad food combination.
conditions: Avoid combining.

reason: Considered a bad food combination.
conditions: Avoid combining.

reason: Cold drinks dampen digestive fire; warm fluids are preferred to support digestion and prevent fat accumulation.
conditions: Always avoided in favor of warm fluids.

food: Tea and Coffee outside the evening window, or on an empty stomach
reason: Caffeine on an empty stomach is discouraged; tea/coffee should be reserved for the evening with minimal sugar.
conditions: Limited to evening only, max 100ml/day combined, never on empty stomach.

reason: Only local dairy farm milk should be used; packaged or low-fat 'slim' milk is not allowed.
conditions: Always restricted in favor of fresh local dairy milk.

reason: Foods should be fresh and 'filled with life'; reheated/leftover preparations are discouraged.
conditions: General principle, applies broadly.

food: Rajma, Chana, or Kadhi (Week 3 dinner specifically)
reason: Specific weekly exclusion noted in the Week 3 dinner plan ('don't have rajma, chana or kadhi this week').
conditions: Restricted specifically for Week 3 dinners only; permitted in other weeks.

reason: Avoided as evening fruit snacks on weeks where specified, likely due to higher sugar content.
conditions: Restricted specifically as evening/mid-morning snack fruits on certain weeks (e.g. Weeks 5, 7); not a blanket restriction across the whole program.

reason: Restricted per Week 9 instructions alongside potato and white rice.
conditions: Restricted in Week 9; general caution elsewhere as part of high-sugar fruit list.
